item.title: Stanford CS336 | Language Modeling from Scratch
item.description: Official course website for Stanford CS336: Language Modeling from Scratch (Spring 2026), including logistics, schedule, assignments, and course materials.

item.title: DuckDuckGo makes its 'no-AI' search engine easier to access as its traffic booms | TechCrunch
item.description: Alternative search engine DuckDuckGo launches 'no AI' web extensions for Chrome and Firefox users.

item.title: The web is changing, and we are not going back
item.description: Whenever I saw someone type a natural language query into Google, it made me cringe. "It's not a person," I would say. "Type like you're talking to a machine." This was especially true for programmers

item.title: The art and engineering of Silpheed
Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
item.description: Anthropic has confidentially submitted a draft S-1 registration statement to the Securities and Exchange Commission

item.title: The Newest Instagram "Exploit" is the Goofiest I've Seen
item.description: Yesterday, a slew of Instagram accounts, including some high profile ones like the Obama White House account, seemingly got hacked. I've seen my share of exploits and takeover techniques, but this is the most unserious, "almost too stupid to be true" of them all.

item.title: A 10 year old Xeon is all you need - point.free
item.description: Or running Gemma 4 on a 2016 Xeon with no GPU, 25 flags, 128 GB of DDR3, and a 25B-parameter MoE.

item.title: Chuwi Minibook X: the netbook we deserve
item.description: Netbooks are dead, but the Chuwi Minibook X scratches the same itch.

item.title: Weird projects I shipped with AI
item.description: Where are all the AI-generated projects? This is a common question from AI skeptics: if LLMs are so good at writing code, where is the tsunami of new AI-generated apps, services and games?

item.title: PrismML — Introducing 1-bit and Ternary Bonsai Image 4B: Image Generation for Local Devices
